_comment: Declarative routing table for the shared `tsp-spector verify-surface-checks` engine. AUTHORED once per emitter (by an agent or a human) — NOT generated by the emitter build, and NOT language-specific code: the engine is language-agnostic, all the Python specifics live here as file globs + regex templates + a casing map. A category listed here is decided without AI; one not listed is verified by AI. Placeholders in `find`/`scope`: {target}=spec target, {name}=resolved client name (client_names[language] or details.name), and any details key ({base},{client},{absentFrom},...). Add `:casing` to recase, e.g. {name:byKind} (per-kind map below), {target:snake}. `expect`: present (default), absent, or {absentWhen:<detailKey>}.
_promotable: A category with a routine here is deterministic; leaving it OUT makes it AI-verified until added. Adding an entry (a pattern like `hierarchy` below) flips its checks from AI to deterministic with no change to the spec assertion. An agent can author these entries from context.md.
_test_verifier_example: Instead of a pattern, a category may defer to an existing test that already asserts this surface: { "exactName": { "test": "emitter-tests/src/test/java/.../ExactNameTests.java#L34-L35", "note": "reflection asserts the exact generated name" } }. Reported status 'deferred', how 'test' (no pattern, no AI).
_not_applicable_example: If a surface concept has no equivalent in this language, mark the whole category N/A (mirrors wire `unsupportedScenarios`): { "hierarchy": { "na": true, "note": "no class inheritance in this language" } }. Every check in the category is reported status 'not-applicable', how 'not-applicable' (no pattern, no AI).
